certifications.name: Google Associate Cloud Engineer (ACE)
certifications.description: Validates your ability to deploy applications, monitor operations, manage enterprise solutions, and configure networks and security on GCP.

cheatsheet.summary: Essential practical blueprint for setting up, configuring, deploying, monitoring, and securing GCP environments. Focuses on core infrastructure, container hosting, network topology, and security policies.

coreConcepts.name: IAM Hierarchy & Bindings
coreConcepts.desc: Organization -> Folder -> Project -> Resource. Access is granted via bindings: Member + Role (Primitive, Predefined, Custom) = Access. Principles prioritize least privilege and group-based assignments.

coreConcepts.name: VPC Global Network
coreConcepts.desc: VPCs are global resources. Subnets within them are regional. Internal VMs can communicate across regions natively using private IP addresses over Google's backplane without public internet routing.

coreConcepts.name: Managed Instance Groups (MIGs)
coreConcepts.desc: Utilizes instance templates to auto-scale VM instances based on CPU/traffic load, perform auto-healing via HTTP health checks, and support rolling updates with zero downtime.

coreConcepts.name: Cloud Storage Classes
coreConcepts.desc: Standard (frequent access), Nearline (accessed < once/30 days, backup storage), Coldline (accessed < once/90 days, archive), Archive (accessed < once/365 days, regulatory compliance/DR).

coreConcepts.name: Serverless & Compute Options
coreConcepts.desc: Compute Engine (IaaS VMs), GKE (hybrid/Kubernetes orchestration), App Engine (PaaS runtime web hosting), Cloud Run (serverless containerized app hosting), Cloud Functions (FaaS event-driven).

coreConcepts.name: GKE Pods & Services
coreConcepts.desc: Pods are smallest deployable units. Services define network access: ClusterIP (internal), NodePort (exposes on VM ports), LoadBalancer (provisions External Load Balancer), Ingress (Layer-7 routing).

coreConcepts.name: Cloud SQL vs Cloud Spanner
coreConcepts.desc: Cloud SQL represents regional relational databases (up to 64TB, supports MySQL/Postgres/SQL Server). Cloud Spanner provides globally scale-out relational transactions with strong consistency.

coreConcepts.name: Operations Suite (Stackdriver)
coreConcepts.desc: Cloud Logging (aggregates system logs, routes logs via Log Sinks to BigQuery, GCS, or Pub/Sub), Cloud Monitoring (metrics, dashboards, and triggers alerting policies).

commands.desc: Configures and locks the active command-line context to the specified project ID.
Provisions a virtual machine instance in Compute Engine with specified configurations.
Initializes a managed Kubernetes GKE cluster with N nodes per zone.
Creates a Cloud Storage bucket using the specified class and geographic region location.
Runs an analytical query using standard SQL compiler inside BigQuery datasets.

architecturalPatterns.scenario: Safe Internet Egress for Private VMs
architecturalPatterns.solution: Deploy VMs in a private subnet (no external IPs). Route outgoing traffic (e.g. for software updates) through a Cloud Router and Cloud NAT gateway.
Store static website media in a Cloud Storage bucket with uniform access. Front the bucket with a Global HTTP(S) Load Balancer and enable Cloud CDN for low-latency caching.
Frontend instances write task payloads to a Pub/Sub queue. An asynchronous backend worker running on Cloud Run consumes the events and writes output to Firestore.

questions.question: [IAM Roles Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your development team needs to store and retrieve files from a Cloud Storage bucket. The team should be able to view, list, delete, and overwrite objects, but must not be allowed to change bucket permissions. Which IAM role should you grant?
questions.explanation: Storage Object Admin allows full control over Cloud Storage objects (files), but does not allow modifying bucket-level IAM policies or permissions. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-associate-cloud-engineer-q1)

questions.question: [Compute Engine Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to set up autoscaling for a containerized web application running on Compute Engine VMs. What resource type must you create first to enable autoscaling?
questions.explanation: Compute Engine autoscaling is a feature of Managed Instance Groups (MIGs). You cannot configure autoscaling on unmanaged instance groups. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-associate-cloud-engineer-q2)

questions.question: [Cloud Storage Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which Cloud Storage class is most cost-effective for data that you plan to access less than once a year?
questions.explanation: Archive Storage is the lowest-cost, highly durable storage service for data archiving, online backup, and disaster recovery. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-associate-cloud-engineer-q3)

questions.question: [VPC Networking Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You create a new custom VPC network. Within this network, you create two subnets in different regions. What is the default internal connectivity status between VMs in these subnets?
options: They cannot communicate internally because they are in different regions.
They can communicate internally by default because Google Cloud custom VPCs have system-generated routing rules that connect all subnets in the VPC.
They can only communicate internally if you set up VPC Network Peering.
They can only communicate if they are assigned public external IP addresses.
questions.explanation: In Google Cloud, VPC networks are global. Subnets within the same VPC, regardless of their regions, are automatically connected via system-generated internal routes. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-associate-cloud-engineer-q4)

questions.question: [Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to deploy a containerized application to GKE and ensure that Google automatically manages the node upgrading, scaling, and provisioning. Which GKE cluster mode should you select?
questions.explanation: In Autopilot mode, GKE provisions and manages the cluster's underlying infrastructure, including nodes, scaling, security, and upgrades. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-associate-cloud-engineer-q5)

certifications.name: Google Cloud Digital Leader
certifications.description: Validates foundational knowledge of cloud concepts, Google Cloud products, services, tools, features, and primary use cases.

cheatsheet.summary: Business-focused blueprint validating foundational cloud concepts, shared responsibility principles, Google Cloud resource hierarchies, and business cases for database, AI, and analytic migrations.

coreConcepts.name: CapEx vs OpEx Shift
coreConcepts.desc: Moving from Capital Expenditure (upfront investments in physical servers, power, real estate) to Operational Expenditure (pay-as-you-go elastic utility model matching demand).

coreConcepts.name: IaaS vs PaaS vs SaaS
coreConcepts.desc: Infrastructure (Compute Engine, Disks where you manage OS), Platform (App Engine, Cloud Run where Google manages OS/runtimes), Software (Google Workspace/packaged web apps).

coreConcepts.name: Shared Responsibility Model
coreConcepts.desc: Google manages security 'of' the cloud (physical centers, network fibers, hardware virtualizations). The customer manages security 'in' the cloud (IAM controls, database schemas, firewall configurations).

coreConcepts.name: GCP Resource Hierarchy
coreConcepts.desc: Enforces structure: Organization -> Folders -> Projects -> Resources. Inherits permissions down the hierarchy, enabling automated billing and compliance constraints.

coreConcepts.name: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO)
coreConcepts.desc: Calculated by comparing cloud operational fees against baseline on-premises expenses (cooling, electricity, physical space, hardware refreshes, network maintenance staffs).

coreConcepts.name: Big Data and AI Offerings
coreConcepts.desc: BigQuery (serverless data warehouse for analytical SQL), Looker (business intelligence visualization dashboards), Vertex AI (unified AI development platform).

coreConcepts.name: Cloud Storage & Databases
coreConcepts.desc: Cloud Storage (object storage), Cloud SQL (managed SQL), Cloud Spanner (global scale relational), Firestore (NoSQL document).

commands.desc: Interactive web utility used to estimate monthly infrastructure pricing based on expected usage patterns.
Configure budget targets and email triggers in Billing Console to prevent unexpected cloud invoice spikes.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Migrate on-premises Windows/Linux servers directly to Compute Engine VMs (lift-and-shift) to eliminate datacenter maintenance.
Load sales transaction feeds into BigQuery, aggregate metrics using SQL, and build interactive dashboards using Looker for business stakeholders.

questions.question: [Cloud Concepts Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: What is the primary benefit of shifting from Capital Expenditure (CapEx) to Operational Expenditure (OpEx) in cloud computing?
options: Paying a fixed amount regardless of usage
Paying only for resources consumed, reducing upfront hardware costs

certifications.name: Google Cloud Generative AI Leader
certifications.description: Validates foundational knowledge of Generative AI concepts, responsible AI development, and deploying gen AI solutions strategically using Vertex AI, Gemini, and Agent Builder.

cheatsheet.summary: Strategic overview of Generative AI capabilities, prompt engineering strategies, RAG architecture, responsible AI evaluations, and Vertex AI enterprise application builder tools.

coreConcepts.name: Generative vs Discriminative AI
coreConcepts.desc: Generative AI produces new data instances (text, images, audio, code) by learning underlying patterns, whereas Discriminative AI classifies inputs or predicts labels.

coreConcepts.name: Foundation Models & LLMs
coreConcepts.desc: Massive deep learning neural networks (like Gemini) pre-trained on diverse datasets, capable of reasoning, translation, and adaptation to custom tasks.

coreConcepts.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
coreConcepts.desc: Architectural design that queries external databases or document stores to retrieve context, appending it to the prompt to ground model outputs in factual, real-time data.

coreConcepts.name: Responsible AI & Ethics
coreConcepts.desc: Frameworks for safety auditing, fairness, transparency, and bias reduction under Google Cloud's AI Principles. Employs safety filters to block harmful inputs/outputs.

coreConcepts.name: Prompt Engineering vs Tuning
coreConcepts.desc: Prompt Engineering designs context within input queries. Tuning (e.g. Fine-Tuning, Parameter-Efficient Tuning) updates actual model weights using training data.

coreConcepts.name: Vertex AI Agent Builder
coreConcepts.desc: No-code/low-code platform that lets enterprises build search engines and conversational chatbots grounded in internal PDFs, websites, or database sources.

coreConcepts.name: Gemini for Google Cloud
coreConcepts.desc: Always-on AI collaborator providing code suggestions, log analysis, configuration troubleshooting, and billing guidance inside the GCP console.

commands.desc: Interactive console for prototyping prompts, configuring temperature parameters, and comparing response outputs.
Bind custom agent instances to secure Google Cloud Storage or BigQuery data stores containing internal wikis.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Create a chat agent using Vertex AI Agent Builder, link it to customer documentation stored in GCS, and embed it as a grounded web assistant.
Establish a search engine grounded in internal financial records inside a VPC Service Controls perimeter. Configure safety thresholds to block sensitive outputs.

questions.question: [Responsible AI Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which of the following aligns with Google's Responsible AI principles when deploying generative models?
options: Maximizing the model's creativity regardless of factual accuracy
Ensuring safety evaluations, monitoring bias, and implementing safety filters
Locking model outputs to prevent any variation in response
Sharing user prompts publicly to foster model collaboration
questions.explanation: Responsible AI practices require continuous evaluation for safety, bias tracking, and deploying guardrails/filters to prevent unsafe content generation. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-generative-ai-leader-q1)

questions.question: [Vertex AI Offerings Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your business needs to build a custom AI chatbot that can answer customer queries using your internal company wikis and PDF files, without writing complex machine learning pipelines. Which Google Cloud tool should you select?
options: Compute Engine VMs with manually installed PyTorch
Vertex AI Agent Builder (Search and Conversation)
BigQuery ML with custom linear regression models
questions.explanation: Vertex AI Agent Builder enables rapid construction of enterprise-grade search engines and generative chatbots grounded in custom internal data with minimal code. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-generative-ai-leader-q2)

questions.question: [Generative AI Concepts Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: What is the primary difference between Generative AI and Discriminative AI?
options: Generative AI only processes numerical data, whereas Discriminative AI processes natural text.
Generative AI creates new data instances based on learned patterns, while Discriminative AI classifies existing data.
Generative AI runs only on-premises, while Discriminative AI runs in the cloud.
Discriminative AI uses deep learning, whereas Generative AI does not.
questions.explanation: Generative AI models learn data distributions to generate new, original content (e.g., text, images), whereas Discriminative AI predicts labels or categories by distinguishing between data points. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-generative-ai-leader-q3)

questions.question: [Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your company's LLM frequently outputs outdated or incorrect information about product availability. Which design pattern should you implement to ground the model's responses in real-time inventory databases?
options: Increasing the model's temperature parameter to maximum
Fine-tuning the foundation model every hour
Hardcoding answers inside the prompt itself
questions.explanation: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) queries dynamic external databases to retrieve factual, real-time context and appends it to the user's prompt, reducing hallucinations. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-generative-ai-leader-q4)

certifications.name: Google Professional Cloud Architect (PCA)
certifications.description: Evaluates your ability to design, develop, and manage robust, secure, scalable, and highly available GCP solutions.

cheatsheet.summary: Advanced design blueprint for enterprise environments. Focuses on multi-region high availability, compliance guardrails, disaster recovery, global load balancing, and secure hybrid cloud connectivity.

coreConcepts.name: HA & Disaster Recovery (DR)
coreConcepts.desc: Active-Active configurations distribute request loads. Active-Passive uses failover routes. RTO (recovery speed) and RPO (data loss threshold) dictate storage replication choices.

coreConcepts.name: GCP Global Load Balancing
coreConcepts.desc: Global External HTTP(S) LB (Layer-7 routing, SSL offloading, Cloud Armor, and CDN integration). Network Load Balancers (Layer-4 TCP/UDP, high throughput).

coreConcepts.name: VPC Service Controls Perimeters
coreConcepts.desc: Establishes a security perimeter around multi-tenant APIs (like Cloud Storage or BigQuery) to prevent malicious or accidental data exfiltration.

coreConcepts.name: Enterprise Hybrid Interconnect
coreConcepts.desc: Dedicated Interconnect (direct physical links to Google cores, 10G/100G, highest SLAs). Partner Interconnect (private links via ISPs). HA VPN (99.99% SLA, IPsec over public web).

coreConcepts.name: Governance & Organization Policies
coreConcepts.desc: Define organizational-level rules (e.g., block external IPs on VMs, restrict locations to US/Europe, enforce Shared VPC structures across folders).

coreConcepts.name: Databases at Scale
coreConcepts.desc: Cloud Spanner (fully managed, relational, synchronous multi-region replication, horizontal scale). Bigtable (low-latency NoSQL time-series). BigQuery (petabyte-scale serverless analytics).

commands.desc: Initiates configuration of a physical direct fiber connection to Google Cloud network edge.
Designates the specified project as the host project to share subnets across tenant service projects.
Applies system-wide organization policies to block insecure defaults (e.g. IAM service account key creations).

architecturalPatterns.solution: Front-end traffic routed via Global HTTP(S) Load Balancer to MIGs in US and Europe. Data tier deployed on a multi-region Cloud Spanner database for global consistency.
Ingest database backups from on-premises over Dedicated Interconnect. Process logs in a Project enclosed in a VPC Service Controls perimeter, storing outputs in BigQuery.
Set up an organizational log sink to export all system and access logs into a central bucket configured with Cloud Storage Object Retention Lock.

questions.question: [Designing for High Availability Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are designing a high-availability multi-tier application. The database layer must support failover with zero data loss across regions. Which architecture should you choose?
options: Cloud SQL with cross-region read replicas promoted manually
Cloud Spanner with a multi-region configuration
Compute Engine VM instances with database replication over Cloud VPN
Cloud SQL with High Availability (HA) enabled in a single region
questions.explanation: Cloud Spanner multi-region configurations provide synchronous replication and database failover with a 99.999% availability SLA and zero data loss. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-architect-q1)

questions.question: [Cloud Migration Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are migrating 100 TB of historical log data from your on-premises SAN to Cloud Storage. The migration must complete within 2 days over a 1 Gbps dedicated internet link. What is the recommended strategy?
options: Upload data using gsutil multi-threading over the internet.
Order a Google Transfer Appliance, load the data on-premises, and ship it to Google.
Configure a Cloud VPN connection and copy the files.
Use Partner Interconnect to transfer the data.
questions.explanation: A 1 Gbps connection would take around 10-12 days to upload 100 TB. The Google Transfer Appliance is the recommended, secure offline data transfer option for this scale and timeframe. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-architect-q2)

questions.question: [Security and Compliance Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your enterprise organization needs to prevent data exfiltration from Cloud Storage buckets and BigQuery datasets, ensuring they are only accessible from within your private network or authorized subnets. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: VPC Service Controls allows you to define a security perimeter around Google Cloud resources to prevent data exfiltration and block unauthorized access. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-architect-q3)

questions.question: [Load Balancing Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You need to distribute global HTTPS traffic to backends located in multiple regions (US, Europe, Asia) to minimize latency for end-users. Which load balancer should you use?
options: Global External HTTP(S) Load Balancer (Classic or Modern)
questions.explanation: The Global External HTTP(S) Load Balancer is a proxy load balancer that routes user requests to the closest backend instance based on geography and capacity. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-architect-q4)

questions.question: [Disaster Recovery Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to configure disaster recovery for VM instances running in us-east1. In the event of a zone outage, the VMs should be bootable in us-east4 with minimal RTO. What is the most cost-effective recovery method?
options: Maintain active duplicate VMs running in us-east4.
Configure schedule-based persistent disk snapshots and replicate them to us-east4.
Export VM disks to Cloud Storage and import them when needed.
Use GKE multi-cluster ingress to duplicate VM instances.
questions.explanation: Replicating persistent disk snapshots across regions provides a low-cost, secure backup of VM states that can be quickly restored as boot disks in another region. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-architect-q5)

certifications.name: Google Professional Data Engineer (PDE)
certifications.description: Assesses skills in designing, building, operationalizing, and securing data processing systems on Google Cloud.

cheatsheet.summary: Data architecture blueprint validating pipeline pipelines, streaming analytics (Dataflow/PubSub), serverless warehouses (BigQuery), time-series databases (Bigtable), and compliance orchestrations.

coreConcepts.name: BigQuery Partitioning & Clustering
coreConcepts.desc: Partitioning splits tables by date or integer range (limits scanned bytes). Clustering sorts data by key columns inside partitions (speeds up grouping/filtering).

coreConcepts.name: Cloud Bigtable Row Key Design
coreConcepts.desc: NoSQL wide-column time-series store. Avoid sequential keys (e.g. timestamp first) to prevent hotspotting. Design row keys as sensor_id#timestamp to group queries.

coreConcepts.name: Dataflow Windowing & Stream
coreConcepts.desc: Based on Apache Beam. Tumbling (fixed non-overlapping), Hopping (overlapping), Session (inactivity gap). Watermarks track event-time vs processing-time progression.

coreConcepts.name: Composer Workflow Orchestration
coreConcepts.desc: Fully managed Apache Airflow. Runs Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) in Python to automate ETL pipeline steps, coordinate Dataproc Spark jobs, and trigger BigQuery updates.

coreConcepts.name: Data Security & DLP masking
coreConcepts.desc: Utilize Cloud Data Loss Prevention (DLP) API to scan, identify, and redact PII data (e.g., credit card numbers, SSNs) inside streaming pipelines before database writes.

commands.desc: Creates a structured date-partitioned BigQuery table with resource expiration policies.
Executes analytical SQL commands against BigQuery datasets using standard SQL engine.
Deploys a managed Apache Spark/Hadoop processing cluster on compute nodes.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Stream web events to Pub/Sub, compute metrics using sliding windows in Dataflow, and stream directly into BigQuery for visualization.
Ingest millisecond sensor data into Pub/Sub, parse and filter metrics in Dataflow, and write outputs to Bigtable using a row key structure of 'sensor_id#timestamp'.
Upload files to a staging Cloud Storage bucket. Trigger a Dataflow job that calls the DLP API to mask PII data, and load the anonymized records into BigQuery.

questions.question: [BigQuery Optimization Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You run heavy analytical queries in BigQuery that filter by a transaction date and group by a store ID. How should you design the table to minimize costs and optimize query speed?
options: Partition by transaction date and cluster by store ID.
Cluster by transaction date and partition by store ID.
Create separate tables for every store ID.
Increase the reservation slots in BigQuery.
questions.explanation: Partitioning by date narrows down the scanned data blocks (reducing cost), while clustering by store ID sorts data within partitions to speed up grouping and filtering. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-data-engineer-q1)

questions.question: [Bigtable Schema Design Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are designing a row key for a Cloud Bigtable database storing IoT sensor metrics. The query pattern is always retrieving records for a specific sensor ID over a recent time range. How should you format the row key to avoid hotspotting?
questions.explanation: Starting the row key with the sensor ID groups that sensor's metrics together. Appending the timestamp allows time-range queries. Placing timestamp first would cause hotspotting on the node handling the current time. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-data-engineer-q2)

questions.question: [Data Pipeline Orchestration Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You need to orchestrate a complex workflow that extracts data from Cloud Storage, triggers a Dataproc Spark job, and loads results into BigQuery daily. Which Google Cloud service should you use?
questions.explanation: Cloud Composer is a fully managed workflow orchestration service built on Apache Airflow, ideal for authoring, scheduling, and monitoring pipelines. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-data-engineer-q3)

questions.question: [Stream Processing Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are building a real-time data streaming pipeline that needs to ingest data from mobile apps, perform aggregations over a sliding window, and handle late-arriving data. Which service combination should you select?
options: Pub/Sub for ingestion and Cloud Dataflow for processing
Pub/Sub for ingestion and BigQuery for processing
Cloud Storage for ingestion and Dataproc for processing
Cloud SQL for ingestion and Cloud Composer for processing
questions.explanation: Pub/Sub handles scalable ingestion, while Cloud Dataflow (based on Apache Beam) supports streaming windowing, watermarks, and triggers for late data. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-data-engineer-q4)

questions.question: [Database Selection Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your application requires a NoSQL document database that supports real-time synchronization, mobile offline access, and ACID transactions. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Firestore is a serverless NoSQL document database designed for mobile and web apps, supporting ACID transactions and offline sync. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-data-engineer-q5)

certifications.name: Google Professional Cloud Developer (PCD)
certifications.description: Tests your proficiency in designing, building, testing, and deploying cloud-native applications on Google Cloud.

cheatsheet.summary: Development blueprint focusing on serverless app design (Cloud Run/Functions), API authentication, CI/CD orchestration, distributed tracing, profiling, and secure configuration management.

coreConcepts.name: Serverless Scaling & Runtimes
coreConcepts.desc: Cloud Run runs containerized microservices, scaling down to zero when idle. Set minimum instances to prevent cold starts. Adjust concurrency settings to optimize throughput per instance.

coreConcepts.name: CI/CD & Release Management
coreConcepts.desc: Automated pipelines: Cloud Build compiles code, Artifact Registry stores images securely, and Cloud Deploy coordinates canary or blue-green releases to GKE or Cloud Run.

coreConcepts.name: Distributed Trace & Monitoring
coreConcepts.desc: Cloud Trace logs HTTP request latencies across microservice boundaries. Cloud Profiler monitors CPU and memory usage in code. Cloud Logging captures console outputs.

coreConcepts.name: API Gateways & Security
coreConcepts.desc: Expose microservices using API Gateway or Apigee for request throttling, API key verification, OAuth authentication, and cataloging REST resources.

coreConcepts.name: Secrets Management
coreConcepts.desc: Store sensitive keys (API tokens, passwords) in Secret Manager. Grant the compute service account Secret Manager Viewer access, and inject secrets as env variables at startup.

commands.desc: Triggers a build on Cloud Build and substitutes variables dynamically.
Reads the raw payload of the latest active secret version from the command-line.
Deploys service to Cloud Run with customized scaling and concurrency configurations.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Deploy a Node.js microservice to Cloud Run. Cache frequent database query outputs in Cloud Memorystore (Redis) to decrease response latencies.
Submit transactions to API Gateway. Write records to Pub/Sub to decouple services, and process transactions asynchronously via a worker container on Cloud Run.

questions.question: [CI/CD Pipelines Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to automate your application's deployment pipeline. Code changes pushed to GitHub should automatically build a container image, push it to Artifact Registry, and deploy it to Cloud Run. Which tool should you use to coordinate this?
questions.explanation: Cloud Build is Google Cloud's serverless CI/CD platform that compiles code, creates container images, and executes deployment scripts. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-developer-q1)

questions.question: [Application Debugging Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You have deployed a production microservice to Cloud Run. Users are experiencing intermittent timeouts. You want to trace requests across microservices to identify bottlenecks. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Cloud Trace is a distributed tracing system that collects latency data from your applications and displays request lifecycles. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-developer-q2)

questions.question: [Secrets Management Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your application needs to securely store API keys and database credentials. You must prevent hardcoding secrets in application source files. Which service should you integrate?
questions.explanation: Secret Manager is a secure, convenient storage system for API keys, passwords, credentials, and other sensitive data. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-developer-q3)

questions.question: [App Engine Configurations Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to deploy a Python web application to App Engine Standard. Which file must you configure in the root directory to define the scaling behavior and runtime environment?
questions.explanation: App Engine Standard uses the `app.yaml` file to define runtime environments, entry points, scaling behaviors, and environment variables. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-developer-q4)

questions.question: [Microservices Ingestion Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are building a decoupled microservice application. Service A needs to trigger Service B asynchronously whenever a user registers. Which service is designed to handle this decoupling?
questions.explanation: Cloud Pub/Sub is an asynchronous messaging service that decouples services that produce events from services that process events. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-developer-q5)

certifications.name: Google Professional Cloud Security Engineer
certifications.description: Tests your ability to design, develop, and manage a secure infrastructure using Google Cloud security technologies.

cheatsheet.summary: Advanced security blueprint covering key management (KMS), resource perimeters (VPC SC), firewall structures, WAF filtering (Cloud Armor), identity proxies (IAP), and sensitive data management.

coreConcepts.name: Encryption at Rest Portfolio
coreConcepts.desc: GMEK (Google-Managed, default), CMEK (Customer-Managed, key generation/lifecycle in Cloud KMS), CSEK (Customer-Supplied, keys managed on-premises).

coreConcepts.name: VPC Service Controls
coreConcepts.desc: Locks down APIs (like GCS or BigQuery) by enclosing projects in service perimeters, preventing network exfiltrations and restricting traffic to verified sources.

coreConcepts.name: Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP)
coreConcepts.desc: Secures web applications running on App Engine, GKE, or VMs without using VPNs, by verifying user identity and context via IAM policy rules.

coreConcepts.name: Cloud Armor & WAF
coreConcepts.desc: Provides Layer-7 firewall filtering, DDoS mitigation, and OWASP Top 10 web attack protection (e.g. blocking SQL injections) at HTTP(S) Load Balancer level.

coreConcepts.name: IAM Governance & Workload Identity
coreConcepts.desc: Workload Identity allows GKE Pods to authenticate directly as GCP Service Accounts, avoiding the need to download or mount static private service account key files.

commands.desc: Initializes a key vault keyring wrapper at a specific location.
Generates a key within the keyring for data encryption purposes.
Creates a firewall rule to allow SSH access only via Cloud IAP TCP forwarding range.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Expose an internal administrative portal running on Compute Engine VMs behind an HTTPS Load Balancer with Identity-Aware Proxy (IAP) enabled, restricting access to verified company users.
Isolate the BigQuery database and raw data storage buckets inside a VPC Service Controls perimeter. Configure an ingress rule to allow access only from an authorized VPC network.
Stream logs to a Pub/Sub topic, execute a Dataflow pipeline that calls the DLP API to mask SSNs and phone numbers, and save the sanitized logs to BigQuery.

questions.question: [Data Encryption Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your company's security policy requires that all data stored in Cloud Storage must be encrypted using keys that you generate, manage, and rotate outside of Google Cloud. Which encryption method should you use?
options: Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK) via Cloud KMS
questions.explanation: CSEKs allow you to supply your own encryption keys. Google uses these keys to encrypt the data, but never stores them on disk. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q1)

questions.question: [Web Application Security Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your web application running on Compute Engine VMs is experiencing Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and SQL injection attacks from the internet. Which service should you configure to protect it?
questions.explanation: Cloud Armor protects web applications from DDoS attacks and OWASP Top 10 risks (like SQL injection and cross-site scripting). (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q2)

questions.question: [Identity and Access Management Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to grant users access to an administrative VM instance without exposing its public IP address or configuring a VPN. Which security service should you implement?
questions.explanation: IAP's TCP forwarding feature allows you to control who can access administrative services (like SSH or RDP) on VMs without public IP addresses. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q3)

questions.question: [Sensitive Data Discovery Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to automatically scan Cloud Storage buckets for personally identifiable information (PII) like credit card numbers or social security IDs, and redact them. Which tool should you use?
questions.explanation: Sensitive Data Protection (formerly Cloud DLP) helps you discover, classify, and redact sensitive data in cloud storage and databases. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q4)

questions.question: [Auditing and Logging Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You need to audit all read actions on files inside your Cloud Storage buckets. By default, these logs are disabled to save storage costs. Which log type must you enable?
questions.explanation: Data Access Audit logs record when API calls read resource configurations or user-provided data (disabled by default, except for BigQuery). (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q5)

questions.explanation: CSEKs allow you to supply your own encryption keys. Google uses these keys to encrypt the data, but never stores them on disk. (Difficulty: Medium, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q31)

questions.question: [Web Application Security Scenario 7] In your company's Project G environment: Your web application running on Compute Engine VMs is experiencing Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and SQL injection attacks from the internet. Which service should you configure to protect it?
questions.explanation: Cloud Armor protects web applications from DDoS attacks and OWASP Top 10 risks (like SQL injection and cross-site scripting). (Difficulty: Medium, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q32)

questions.question: [Identity and Access Management Scenario 7] In your company's Project G environment: You want to grant users access to an administrative VM instance without exposing its public IP address or configuring a VPN. Which security service should you implement?
questions.explanation: IAP's TCP forwarding feature allows you to control who can access administrative services (like SSH or RDP) on VMs without public IP addresses. (Difficulty: Medium, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q33)

questions.question: [Sensitive Data Discovery Scenario 7] In your company's Project G environment: You want to automatically scan Cloud Storage buckets for personally identifiable information (PII) like credit card numbers or social security IDs, and redact them. Which tool should you use?
questions.explanation: Sensitive Data Protection (formerly Cloud DLP) helps you discover, classify, and redact sensitive data in cloud storage and databases. (Difficulty: Medium, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q34)

questions.question: [Auditing and Logging Scenario 7] In your company's Project G environment: You need to audit all read actions on files inside your Cloud Storage buckets. By default, these logs are disabled to save storage costs. Which log type must you enable?
questions.explanation: Data Access Audit logs record when API calls read resource configurations or user-provided data (disabled by default, except for BigQuery). (Difficulty: Medium, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-security-engineer-q35)

certifications.name: Google Professional Cloud Network Engineer
certifications.description: Validates your expertise in designing, planning, and managing Google Cloud network architectures.

cheatsheet.summary: Advanced network engineering blueprint. Focuses on VPC architectures, Shared VPC design, hybrid routes (Interconnect/VPN), BGP configurations, private endpoint resolutions, and CDN caching.

coreConcepts.name: Shared VPC Architecture
coreConcepts.desc: Shares VPC subnets from a central Host Project to multiple Service Projects, enforcing administrative network segregation while enabling shared compute connectivity.

coreConcepts.name: VPC Network Peering
coreConcepts.desc: Connects two VPC networks internally with low latency and high bandwidth across distinct organizations, without using public gateway hops.

coreConcepts.name: Hybrid Connectivities
coreConcepts.desc: Dedicated Interconnect (direct physical fiber, 10G/100G). Partner Interconnect (private ISP circuit, 50M-10G). HA VPN (99.99% SLA, IPsec over public web, dual tunnels).

coreConcepts.name: Dynamic Routing with BGP
coreConcepts.desc: Uses Cloud Router to exchange routing tables dynamically using Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) over VPN or Interconnect links to on-premises routers.

coreConcepts.name: Private Google Access
coreConcepts.desc: Enables VM instances that only have internal IP addresses in a subnet to reach global Google APIs (like BigQuery, GCS) securely without public internet routes.

commands.desc: Creates a custom-mode VPC network without auto-generated default regional subnets.
Creates a subnet and enables Private Google Access.
Deploys a Cloud Router with a BGP Autonomous System Number (ASN).

architecturalPatterns.solution: Deploy all subnets, firewalls, and Interconnect lines in a Shared VPC host project. Share subnets with application-specific service projects for isolation.
Establish a VPN tunnel to AWS. Configure Cloud DNS forwarding zones in GCP to route requests for '.aws' domains to AWS Route53 resolvers.

questions.question: [Hybrid Connectivity Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You need to configure dynamic routing between your on-premises network and a Google Cloud VPC using Cloud VPN. Which service is required to establish BGP sessions?
questions.explanation: Cloud Router uses Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) to dynamically exchange routes between your VPC and on-premises networks. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q1)

questions.question: [VPC Organization Architecture Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your enterprise wants to centralize network management (subnets, firewalls, routing) in a single host project while allowing developers to launch VMs in separate service projects. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Shared VPC allows an organization to connect resources from multiple projects to a common Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q2)

questions.question: [IP Address Management Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You have VM instances in a private subnet (no public IPs) that need to download updates from the public internet. Which service should you configure to enable this internet egress?
questions.explanation: Cloud NAT (Network Address Translation) allows VM instances without external IP addresses to access the internet. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q3)

questions.question: [Load Balancing Traffic Routing Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are hosting a video streaming service. You want to cache content at edge locations to minimize latency for global clients. Which load balancing feature should you enable?
questions.explanation: Cloud CDN (Content Delivery Network) works with external HTTP(S) load balancers to cache media content close to users. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q4)

questions.question: [Domain Name Resolution Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to forward DNS queries from your on-premises active directory domain to your private Google Cloud DNS zones. What should you configure?
options: A hosts file on the instances
questions.explanation: An Inbound DNS Query Policy allows you to configure entry-point IP addresses in your VPC that resolve queries via Cloud DNS. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q5)

questions.question: [VPC Organization Architecture Scenario 10] In your company's Project J environment: Your enterprise wants to centralize network management (subnets, firewalls, routing) in a single host project while allowing developers to launch VMs in separate service projects. What should you configure?
questions.explanation: Shared VPC allows an organization to connect resources from multiple projects to a common Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) network. (Difficulty: Hard, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q47)

questions.question: [IP Address Management Scenario 10] In your company's Project J environment: You have VM instances in a private subnet (no public IPs) that need to download updates from the public internet. Which service should you configure to enable this internet egress?
questions.explanation: Cloud NAT (Network Address Translation) allows VM instances without external IP addresses to access the internet. (Difficulty: Hard, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q48)

questions.question: [Load Balancing Traffic Routing Scenario 10] In your company's Project J environment: You are hosting a video streaming service. You want to cache content at edge locations to minimize latency for global clients. Which load balancing feature should you enable?
questions.explanation: Cloud CDN (Content Delivery Network) works with external HTTP(S) load balancers to cache media content close to users. (Difficulty: Hard, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q49)

questions.question: [Domain Name Resolution Scenario 10] In your company's Project J environment: You want to forward DNS queries from your on-premises active directory domain to your private Google Cloud DNS zones. What should you configure?
options: A hosts file on the instances
questions.explanation: An Inbound DNS Query Policy allows you to configure entry-point IP addresses in your VPC that resolve queries via Cloud DNS. (Difficulty: Hard, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-network-engineer-q50)

certifications.name: Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer
certifications.description: Evaluates proficiency in using Google Cloud services to build and operationalize software delivery pipelines and manage service reliability.

cheatsheet.summary: DevOps and SRE blueprint validating deployment automations, CI/CD canary strategies, SLI/SLO metric configurations, error budget management, logging routers, and IaC provisioning.

coreConcepts.name: SLIs, SLOs, and SLAs
coreConcepts.desc: SLI (Service Level Indicator, e.g. latency). SLO (Service Level Objective, e.g. latency < 200ms for 99.9% of calls). SLA (Service Level Agreement, legal contract with penalties).

coreConcepts.name: Error Budget Management
coreConcepts.desc: The acceptable level of failure (100% - SLO). Used to balance feature deployment speed against system stability. Budget depletion pauses deployments.

coreConcepts.name: Canary & Blue-Green Releases
coreConcepts.desc: Canary rolls out updates to a small subset of servers or users first. Blue-Green switches traffic instantly from green (old) to blue (new) target groups.

coreConcepts.name: Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
coreConcepts.desc: Using Terraform to define, version, and provision GCP infrastructure. Prevents configuration drift and automates environment setups.

coreConcepts.name: Logging Routers & Sinks
coreConcepts.desc: Routes logs from projects, folders, or organizations to BigQuery for SQL analysis, Pub/Sub for real-time alerting, or GCS for long-term audit storage.

commands.desc: Initializes and provisions declared resources using local state configurations.
Initiates a release deployment process using Cloud Deploy delivery configurations.
Creates a Log Sink to export error logs to BigQuery.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Configure Cloud Deploy targeting GKE. Deploy code to 10% target. Monitor SLIs/SLOs in Cloud Monitoring. Auto-promote to 100% if SLOs remain intact.
Manage resources in Git. A merge to main triggers Cloud Build to run Terraform plan, output audits to Cloud Logging, and execute terraform apply.

questions.question: [Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are configuring alerts for a web service. You want to measure the percentage of successful HTTP requests over a 30-day window to ensure reliability. What is this metric called in SRE?
questions.explanation: An SLI is a quantifiable metric of service performance (like successful request ratio). The target (like 99.9%) is the SLO. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-devops-engineer-q1)

questions.question: [Monitoring and Alerting Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to notify your on-call team via PagerDuty whenever your application's error rate exceeds 5% for consecutive minutes. Where should you configure this?
questions.explanation: Cloud Monitoring alerting policies allow you to define metric thresholds and direct notifications to PagerDuty, email, or Slack. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-devops-engineer-q2)

questions.question: [Deployment Strategies Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to deploy a new version of an application to a subset of users first to verify stability before rolling it out to all users. Which deployment strategy does this represent?
questions.explanation: A canary deployment routes a small percentage of production traffic to the new version to check for errors before full deployment. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-devops-engineer-q3)

questions.question: [Infrastructure as Code Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to provision, manage, and version-control your Google Cloud infrastructure in a declarative template format. What is the industry-standard tool supported by Google Cloud providers?

questions.question: [Log Analysis Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to perform real-time SQL analysis on millions of application logs stored in Cloud Logging. What should you configure?
options: Upgrade the log bucket to use Log Analytics and query it via SQL.
Export logs to a Cloud Storage bucket and run Python scripts.
Filter logs in the Log Explorer UI manually.
Configure a Log Sink to Cloud SQL.
questions.explanation: Log Analytics (powered by BigQuery technology) allows you to search, query, and analyze logs using SQL directly in the Logging console. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-cloud-devops-engineer-q5)

certifications.name: Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer
certifications.description: Tests your ability to design, build, and productionize ML models on GCP using Vertex AI.

cheatsheet.summary: ML engineering blueprint focusing on model design, distributed training, pipeline integrations (Vertex AI), prediction endpoints, MLOps, and model drift tracking.

coreConcepts.name: Vertex AI Pipelines & SDK
coreConcepts.desc: Coordinates ML workflows using Kubeflow Pipelines (KFP) or TensorFlow Extended (TFX) to orchestrate data loading, training, and evaluations.

coreConcepts.name: Model Training Architectures
coreConcepts.desc: Custom containers run custom model code. Distributed training scales across multiple compute instances using GPUs or Tensor Processing Units (TPUs).

coreConcepts.name: Model Deployments & Serving
coreConcepts.desc: Deploys model files to Vertex AI Endpoints for low-latency online predictions, or triggers Batch Prediction jobs for processing high-volume datasets.

coreConcepts.name: MLOps, Skew, and Drift Monitoring
coreConcepts.desc: Skew is the difference between training and serving data distributions. Drift is the change in serving data distributions over time. Vertex AI monitors both.

coreConcepts.name: Feature Store Management
coreConcepts.desc: Vertex AI Feature Store provides a centralized repository to store, share, and serve machine learning features, ensuring feature consistency between training and online serving.

commands.desc: Submits a custom model training container job to Vertex AI.
Sends live feature inputs to a deployed Vertex AI model endpoint for online predictions.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Run a daily pipeline in Vertex AI Pipelines that extracts transactional data from BigQuery, processes features, trains an XGBoost model, and registers it in the Model Registry.
Deploy model to an Endpoint. Configure Vertex AI Model Monitoring to sample live inference traffic and send email alerts if feature drift thresholds are breached.

questions.question: [Vertex AI Pipelines Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to orchestrate your machine learning workflow (data preprocessing, training, evaluation, deployment) in a serverless, reproducible pipeline. Which service should you choose?
questions.explanation: Vertex AI Pipelines is a serverless orchestration service that runs ML pipelines using Kubeflow Pipelines or TFX. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-machine-learning-engineer-q1)

questions.question: [Model Training Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You have a large tabular dataset and want to quickly find the best ML model architecture without writing custom TensorFlow or PyTorch training code. Which Vertex AI feature should you use?
questions.explanation: Vertex AI AutoML trains tabular, image, text, or video models automatically without requiring manual architecture design. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-machine-learning-engineer-q2)

questions.question: [Feature Management Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to store, share, and serve machine learning features across multiple training and online serving systems to avoid training-serving skew. Which Vertex AI service is designed for this?
questions.explanation: Vertex AI Feature Store provides a centralized repository for organizing, storing, and serving ML features. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-machine-learning-engineer-q3)

questions.question: [Model Monitoring Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Your deployed ML model's prediction accuracy has degraded over time because the distribution of incoming inference data has changed compared to the training data. What is this phenomenon called?
questions.explanation: Data Drift occurs when input data properties change over time, resulting in degraded model performance (monitored by Vertex AI Model Monitoring). (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-machine-learning-engineer-q4)

questions.question: [Data Ingestion Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are training a deep learning model on petabytes of image data. You want to stream data from Cloud Storage into your training jobs efficiently to prevent GPU starvation. Which data format is recommended?
questions.explanation: The TFRecord format is a simple record-oriented binary format optimized for high-throughput streaming pipelines in TensorFlow. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: google-professional-machine-learning-engineer-q5)

questions.question: [Vertex AI Pipelines Scenario 2] In your company's Project B environment: You want to orchestrate your machine learning workflow (data preprocessing, training, evaluation, deployment) in a serverless, reproducible pipeline. Which service should you choose?

certifications.name: dbt Certified Developer
certifications.description: Validates expertise in analytics engineering workflows, dbt compilation, modeling, testing, documentation, and deployment.

cheatsheet.summary: Advanced software engineering practices with dbt. Covers dynamic Jinja scripting, packages manager, CI/CD slim runs, custom schema architectures, and metadata queries.

coreConcepts.name: Advanced Jinja & Adapter Macros
coreConcepts.desc: Using Jinja control flow and loop structures. Utilizing adapter methods like 'adapter.dispatch()' to run distinct SQL based on database engine.

coreConcepts.name: dbt Package Management
coreConcepts.desc: Imports open-source packages (e.g., dbt_utils) by declaring dependencies in 'packages.yml' and running install commands to import macros.

coreConcepts.name: Slim CI/CD Deployments
coreConcepts.desc: dbt Cloud CI runs only compile and test modified models and their downstream dependencies using 'state:modified+' options, saving warehouse compute costs.

coreConcepts.name: Custom Schema Configurations
coreConcepts.desc: Override default schema placements. Configure 'generate_schema_name' macro to route staging models to dev schemas and marts to production schemas.

coreConcepts.name: Artifacts & Metadata APIs
coreConcepts.desc: dbt runs compile files like 'manifest.json' and 'run_results.json'. These artifacts can be parsed to audit execution times and model dependencies.

commands.desc: Downloads and installs external dbt packages configured in packages.yml.
Validates database connection credentials, profiles file paths, and local project configuration variables.
Runs only modified models and downstream dependencies in CI, deferring unchanged tables to production schemas.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Create a macro that uses 'adapter.dispatch()'. Define different SQL syntaxes for BigQuery and Snowflake to handle date calculations dynamically.
Integrate GitHub Actions with dbt Cloud. A PR run compiles only changed code, deferring to the production manifest to run slim CI checks.

questions.question: [dbt Core Concepts Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: In a dbt project, what programming languages are primarily used to define models?
questions.explanation: dbt combines SQL and Jinja (a templating language) to write modular database transformations. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-developer-q1)

questions.question: [dbt Compilation Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Where does dbt execute the data transformation models when you run 'dbt run'?
options: On the client computer running the dbt CLI
Inside a dedicated dbt cloud server
Directly inside the target data warehouse (e.g., Snowflake, BigQuery)
questions.explanation: dbt does not process data itself. It compiles your code into SQL and sends it to your target database/data warehouse for execution. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-developer-q2)

questions.question: [dbt Materializations Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which dbt materialization configuration compiles the model SQL into a physical table that is completely dropped and rebuilt on every execution?
questions.explanation: The 'table' materialization rebuilds the model as a physical table by dropping the old table and running a CREATE TABLE AS statement. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-developer-q3)

questions.question: [dbt Tests Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which command compiles and executes data tests defined in your dbt project schema files?
questions.explanation: The `dbt test` command runs assertions against your models to validate data quality. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-developer-q4)

questions.question: [dbt Lineage Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: What is the primary function of the ref() function in dbt models?
options: It references external source raw tables.
It establishes lineage dependencies between models, enabling dbt to build models in the correct order.
It executes a sub-query directly from python pandas.
questions.explanation: The `ref()` function allows you to reference other models and tells dbt how to construct the DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) to compile dependencies. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-developer-q5)

certifications.name: dbt Certified Analytics Engineer
certifications.description: Tests advanced competency in structuring warehouses, performance optimization, package management, custom macros, and advanced lineage.

cheatsheet.summary: Analytics engineering study guide focusing on SQL modeling, Jinja macro structures, model materializations, dependencies (ref/source), test configurations, and slowly changing dimensions (Snapshots).

coreConcepts.name: dbt Project Structure
coreConcepts.desc: Configured in 'dbt_project.yml'. Main directories: models/ (SQL files), seeds/ (small static CSVs), macros/ (reusable Jinja functions), analyses/ (one-off SQL).

coreConcepts.name: dbt Materialization Types
coreConcepts.desc: View (default, virtual table), Table (re-built on each run), Incremental (appends new records based on filters), Ephemeral (nested as inline CTEs).

coreConcepts.name: Refs & Sources (DAG Building)
coreConcepts.desc: The ref() function links models, building the Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) for dependencies. The source() function references raw source tables in the data warehouse.

coreConcepts.name: Testing (Schema vs Data)
coreConcepts.desc: Schema tests validate constraints (unique, not_null, accepted_values, relationships) inside YAML files. Data tests are custom SQL files that return failing rows.

coreConcepts.name: dbt Seeds vs Snapshots
coreConcepts.desc: Seeds are static lookup tables compiled from CSV files. Snapshots capture change history over time, implementing SCD Type 2 columns (valid_from, valid_to).

commands.desc: Compiles dbt models into raw SQL and executes them, materializing tables/views in the target data warehouse.
Runs all configured tests against materialized database tables, outputting failures in the console.
Translates dbt Jinja SQL files into raw SQL code, locating outputs in the target/ folder without executing them.

commands.cmd: dbt docs generate && dbt docs serve
commands.desc: Builds documentation JSON files from project yml descriptions and launches a local documentation server.
Runs the specified model and all of its downstream dependencies.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Ingest raw transaction logs. Build staging models (data type casting), intermediate models (aggregating dimensions), and final mart tables for BI tools.
Configure a model with incremental materialization. Use Jinja block 'is_incremental()' to load only rows newer than the max active date in the target table.

questions.question: [Package Management Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to utilize pre-built dbt tests and utility macros (like surrogate_key) created by the dbt community. In which configuration file must you declare these package dependencies?
questions.explanation: dbt packages are declared in a `packages.yml` file in the root of the project, and installed using the `dbt deps` command. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-analytics-engineer-q1)

questions.question: [Custom Macros Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to write reusable Jinja logic to pivot columns or dynamically generate date ranges. Where should you define this code inside your dbt project?
options: In a .sql file in the macros/ directory
In a .yml file in the models/ directory
questions.explanation: Custom dbt macros are written in SQL files within the `macros/` folder using Jinja `{% macro ... %}` syntax. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-analytics-engineer-q2)

questions.question: [Incremental Models Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You are building a high-volume incremental model. You want to avoid duplicate records by overwriting existing rows based on a unique key, utilizing a merge statement. Which incremental strategy configuration should you define?
questions.explanation: The `merge` incremental strategy (supported on warehouses like Snowflake and BigQuery) matches rows using a unique key to update existing records and insert new ones. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-analytics-engineer-q3)

questions.question: [dbt Source Freshness Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: You want to monitor the freshness of your raw source data to ensure your data pipeline has loaded records within the last 6 hours. Which command should you schedule?
questions.explanation: The `dbt source freshness` command uses configurations in your source schema YAML files to query the maximum timestamp of your raw data. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-analytics-engineer-q4)

questions.question: [Custom Materializations Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: What is the correct way to define a custom materialization (e.g. creating index structures or custom tables) in dbt?
options: Use the {% materialization %} block inside a SQL file in your macros/ directory.
Edit the dbt core source code in python.
Configure it directly inside dbt_project.yml under model configs.
Custom materializations are not supported in dbt.
questions.explanation: dbt allows developers to write custom materializations by defining a `{% materialization ... %}` block in a macro SQL file. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: dbt-certified-analytics-engineer-q5)

certifications.name: AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner
certifications.description: Provides a high-level overview of AWS cloud concepts, security, technology, architecture, and billing models.

cheatsheet.summary: Foundational AWS credentials validating core global infrastructure, compute, storage, databases, IAM security models, billing features, and pricing structures.

coreConcepts.name: AWS Global Infrastructure
coreConcepts.desc: Regions (geographical clusters of AZs), Availability Zones (AZs, isolated datacenters), Edge Locations (caching destinations for CloudFront CDN).

coreConcepts.name: Compute Services Portfolio
coreConcepts.desc: Amazon EC2 (IaaS VMs), AWS Lambda (serverless event-driven functions), Amazon ECS/EKS (managed container platforms).

coreConcepts.name: Storage Services Portfolio
coreConcepts.desc: Amazon S3 (object storage), Amazon EBS (block storage volumes for EC2), Amazon EFS (managed shared network file systems).

coreConcepts.name: Databases Portfolio
coreConcepts.desc: Amazon RDS (managed relational DB), Amazon DynamoDB (serverless NoSQL document DB), Amazon Aurora (high-performance relational DB).

coreConcepts.name: AWS Shared Responsibility Model
coreConcepts.desc: AWS manages security 'of' the cloud (datacenter infrastructure). The customer manages security 'in' the cloud (data configurations, IAM users, firewall groups).

coreConcepts.name: AWS IAM Identity Management
coreConcepts.desc: AWS Identity and Access Management. Controls user logins, policies, and permissions. Enforces multi-factor authentication (MFA).

coreConcepts.name: Billing & Budgeting Tools
coreConcepts.desc: AWS Billing Dashboard, AWS Budgets (configures spend alert thresholds), AWS Cost Explorer (analyzes historical usage trends).

commands.desc: Prompts to configure AWS CLI access credentials, default region, and output formatting.
Creates a new Amazon S3 bucket globally.
Launches a basic EC2 instance in the configured subnet.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Deploy an Application Load Balancer to distribute traffic to EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones, backed by an RDS Multi-AZ database.
Store static website files in an S3 bucket. Front the bucket with Amazon CloudFront CDN to cache content globally at edge locations.

questions.question: [AWS Cloud Basics Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: What is the AWS service that provides resizable virtual server instances in the cloud?
questions.explanation: Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) provides secure, resizable compute capacity in the form of virtual servers. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: aws-certified-cloud-practitioner-q1)

questions.question: [AWS Security Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which AWS security tool allows you to control inbound and outbound traffic at the individual Amazon EC2 instance level?
questions.explanation: Security Groups act as a virtual firewall for your EC2 instances to control incoming and outgoing traffic (stateful). (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: aws-certified-cloud-practitioner-q2)

questions.question: [AWS S3 Storage Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which AWS storage service is best suited for storing object data like images, videos, and static files?
questions.explanation: Amazon Simple Storage Service (S3) is an object storage service offering industry-leading scalability and data availability. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: aws-certified-cloud-practitioner-q3)

questions.question: [AWS RDS Database Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: A company wants to deploy a relational SQL database in AWS and offload database patching and backups. Which service should they choose?
options: Amazon EC2 with SQL Server installed
questions.explanation: Amazon RDS handles database administration tasks like patching, backups, and OS updates. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: aws-certified-cloud-practitioner-q4)

questions.question: [AWS Content Delivery Scenario 1] In your company's Project A environment: Which AWS service helps users distribute content globally to end-users with low latency by caching content at edge locations?
questions.explanation: Amazon CloudFront is a fast content delivery network (CDN) service that delivers content globally with low latency. (Difficulty: Easy, Question Ref: aws-certified-cloud-practitioner-q5)

questions.question: [AWS RDS Database Scenario 10] In your company's Project J environment: A company wants to deploy a relational SQL database in AWS and offload database patching and backups. Which service should they choose?

questions.question: [AWS Content Delivery Scenario 10] In your company's Project J environment: Which AWS service helps users distribute content globally to end-users with low latency by caching content at edge locations?

certifications.name: Microsoft Azure Fundamentals (AZ-900)
certifications.description: Covers foundational knowledge of Azure cloud services, security, privacy, compliance, trust, and pricing models.

cheatsheet.summary: Foundational Azure credentials validating core geography, compute structures, Blob storage, Microsoft Entra ID authentication, subscription controls, and resource locks.

coreConcepts.name: Azure Core Geographies
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Regions (geographical areas), Availability Zones (isolated datacenters within a region), Region Pairs (cross-region replication pairs for DR).

coreConcepts.name: Compute & Web Resources
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Virtual Machines (VMs), Azure App Services (PaaS web apps), Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), Azure Virtual Desktop.

coreConcepts.name: Blob & Storage Portfolio
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Blob Storage (unstructured object storage), Azure Files (managed network file shares), Azure Disk Storage (block storage for VMs).

coreConcepts.name: Microsoft Entra ID Security
coreConcepts.desc: Provides identity access management (formerly Azure Active Directory), Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), MFA, and Conditional Access policies.

coreConcepts.name: Azure Governance & Resource Locks
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Policies enforce configuration standards. Resource Locks (CannotDelete, ReadOnly) prevent accidental deletions of critical database resource groups.

coreConcepts.name: Management & Architecture Tools
coreConcepts.desc: Azure Portal, Azure CLI, Azure Cloud Shell, Azure Resource Manager (ARM templates define declarative infrastructure deployments).

commands.desc: Launches a browser session to authenticate and log in the active Azure CLI session.

Provisions a virtual machine inside the resource group.

architecturalPatterns.solution: Declare Virtual Networks, Subnets, and SQL Databases inside an ARM or Bicep template to guarantee consistent deployments across Dev/Prod environments.
Register a PaaS App Service in Microsoft Entra ID. Assign an RBAC Reader role to the service account, and enforce Conditional Access MFA rules.
